expectations.text: Ran the bundled analyze_recording.py script on the recording
expectations.evidence: Script output saved to script_output.txt, analysis references script findings throughout

expectations.text: Identified echo/duplication in the system audio track via autocorrelation
expectations.evidence: Clearly stated 'autocorrelation peaks at consistent lags in the 20-53ms range' with r=0.4752 at 53ms

expectations.text: Reported the approximate echo lag (30-53ms range)
expectations.evidence: Reported exact values: 53.0ms (r=0.4752), 20.9ms (r=0.3412), 31.2ms (r=0.3028). Median ~31ms

expectations.text: Noted that cross-track correlation is near zero (not mic bleed)
expectations.evidence: Stated cross-correlation near zero at all time points (max 0.0164), voice-band coherence 0.0000

expectations.text: Flagged that AEC-processed tracks are over-amplified
expectations.evidence: Stated: 'aec_mic: -8.5 LUFS (was -18.6, now WAY too loud)' and 'LOUDNESS BLOWN OUT'

expectations.text: Reported PESQ and/or STOI scores
expectations.evidence: Reported both: STOI 0.540-0.582 (poor), PESQ WB degrades from 4.10 to 2.49

expectations.text: Provided actionable explanation of echo cause
expectations.evidence: Identified virtual audio processor as likely cause, recommended investigating Krisp/Loopback

expectations.text: Ran the bundled analyze_recording.py script on the recording
expectations.evidence: No access to the skill script. Used ad-hoc ffmpeg/sox/python analysis instead.

expectations.text: Identified echo/duplication in the system audio track via autocorrelation
expectations.evidence: MISDIAGNOSED: Attributed echo to 'speaker-to-mic bleed'. Did not perform autocorrelation on system track.

expectations.text: Reported the approximate echo lag (30-53ms range)
expectations.evidence: Mentioned 44ms track offset but did not measure echo via autocorrelation.

expectations.text: Noted that cross-track correlation is near zero (not mic bleed)
expectations.evidence: Reached opposite conclusion: 'only 1.6-2.7 dB quieter - severe bleed'. Used RMS instead of cross-correlation.

expectations.text: Flagged that AEC-processed tracks are over-amplified
expectations.evidence: Noted mic went from -18.6 to -6.6 LUFS and AEC 'amplified the mic track by ~12 dB'. Flagged +14.1 dBTP clipping.

expectations.text: Reported PESQ and/or STOI scores
expectations.evidence: Did not run PESQ or STOI. No speech quality or intelligibility metrics.

expectations.text: Provided actionable explanation of echo cause
expectations.evidence: Provided workaround but misidentified root cause as speaker-to-mic bleed.

expectations.text: Analysis completed without errors on the short recording
expectations.evidence: Script ran successfully, all sections populated with data

expectations.text: Reported track structure (tracks, sample rates, duration)
expectations.evidence: Reported Track 0: AAC 48kHz stereo 7kbps, Track 1: AAC 48kHz stereo 93kbps, ~17 seconds

expectations.text: Reported loudness levels for available tracks
expectations.evidence: System -35.0 LUFS, mic -57.2 LUFS, AEC system -31.8 LUFS, AEC mic -15.0 LUFS

expectations.text: Gave a clear verdict on whether the audio is clean
expectations.evidence: Clear 'NOT clean' verdict at the top with structured findings

expectations.text: Response was appropriately concise for a quick check request
expectations.evidence: 44 lines, organized into 6 numbered sections with a summary. Reasonable for complexity.

expectations.text: Analysis completed without errors on the short recording

expectations.evidence: System -31.8 LUFS, mic -54.6 LUFS with EBU R128 measurements

expectations.text: Gave a clear verdict on whether the audio is clean

expectations.text: Handled the absence of audio-processed.m4a gracefully

expectations.text: Correctly skipped PESQ/STOI comparison

expectations.text: Still ran the applicable analyses (echo, loudness, spectral, SNR)
expectations.evidence: Ran all applicable: loudness, echo detection, cross-track, spectral, SNR (42dB)

expectations.text: Reported track structure of the raw audio file
expectations.evidence: Track 0: AAC 48kHz stereo 51kbps, Track 1: AAC 48kHz stereo 105kbps, 21s and 19.3s

expectations.text: Provided a meaningful quality summary
expectations.evidence: Clear 'GOOD' verdict with summary table. However, MISSED clipping issue that baseline caught (+12.9 dBFS true peak). Script lacks clipping detection.

notes: with_skill missed clipping (+12.9 dBFS true peak) that baseline caught - script lacks clipping/true-peak detection

expectations.text: Handled the absence of audio-processed.m4a gracefully
expectations.evidence: Noted 'No AEC processing' in the title and recommendations

expectations.text: Correctly skipped PESQ/STOI comparison
expectations.evidence: Did not attempt PESQ/STOI - no comparison pair available

expectations.text: Still ran the applicable analyses (echo, loudness, spectral, SNR)
expectations.evidence: Ran loudness (-46.9 LUFS system, -8.7 LUFS mic), clipping (+12.9 dBTP), channel analysis, silence detection, frequency distribution, dynamic range, SNR

expectations.text: Reported track structure of the raw audio file
expectations.evidence: Very detailed: AAC-LC 48kHz stereo, bitrates, durations, priming frames, sample counts

expectations.text: Provided a meaningful quality summary
expectations.evidence: Clear 'POOR' rating due to severe mic clipping (+12.9 dBFS true peak). This finding the with-skill version MISSED.

notes: Eval 0 (echo detection) shows the biggest skill advantage: 7/7 vs 2/7. Without the script, the baseline misdiagnosed the root cause.
Eval 2 reveals a gap: the skill's script lacks clipping/true-peak detection, causing it to miss severe clipping that the baseline's ad-hoc analysis caught.
The skill is consistently faster and uses fewer tokens (~41% fewer tokens on average), likely because the bundled script handles the heavy analysis.
Eval 1 (conciseness) is a clear skill win: 44 lines vs 165 lines for the same 17-second recording.
